name: ui_source_code_generation
purpose: Generate frontend UI source code from UI module specs. Produces one component per page, a router, shared layout with menu, and an auth guard â€” all derived from ui-catalog.json and ui-{module}.json specs.

change_handling.purpose: When requirements change, regenerate impacted UI artifacts from source specs instead of only validating existing code.
workflow: Detect changed upstream sources since last successful generate_ui_source_code run.
Map each changed source to impacted UI modules/pages/routes/types/api clients.
Regenerate every impacted artifact under config.output.uiSrc.
Do not treat a successful build as proof of functional alignment to changed requirements.
If route/menu/page specs changed, regenerate router and sidebar even when individual page files still compile.

fields.framework: react | vue | angular â€” default react
fields.styling: tailwind | css-modules | styled-components â€” default tailwind
fields.stateClient: tanstack-query | swr | none â€” default tanstack-query
fields.authScheme: jwt-bearer | session | none â€” default jwt-bearer

must_include.dependencies: derive from config.ui (react/vue + router + tanstack-query + axios)

must_include.resolve.alias: @ â†’ src/, plus all @pages, @api, @layout, @context, @router aliases matching tsconfig paths

server.proxy.note: Proxy /api to config.ui.apiProxy (e.g. http://localhost:3000). Do NOT add a rewrite that strips /api â€” the backend routes are registered with the /api prefix.

folder_structure.note: All paths are RELATIVE TO config.output.uiSrc (e.g. /dev/myapp/ui/src). Do NOT write to design/ or any other folder.
folder_structure.api: {config.output.uiSrc}/api/ â€” one .ts file per backend module

generation_order: scaffold files in parent of config.output.uiSrc: package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts if absent

page_generation_rules.dashboard: Render summary cards derived from shows[]. Each card navigates to the target listed in actions[type=navigate].
page_generation_rules.list: Render a table or card list. Columns from shows[]. Add filter controls from filters[]. Each row navigates to detail via actions[type=navigate]. Render emptyState when data is empty.
page_generation_rules.detail: Render all fields as read-only. Add action buttons from actions[]. Confirm modal before any type=confirm action.
page_generation_rules.form: Render a form with one input per field in validation[]. Apply validation rules. Submit via actions[type=submit]. Cancel navigates back.
page_generation_rules.wizard: Render a step indicator. Each step is a separate component. Next/Back buttons. Submit only on final step.
page_generation_rules.modal: Render as a dialog overlay. Confirm/Cancel buttons only. No navigation within the modal.
page_generation_rules.split-view: Left pane = list, right pane = detail or form. Selection in list updates right pane.

api_client_rules: One file per backend module â€” src/api/{kebab-module}.ts
Derive endpoints from module-catalog exposes[] array
All calls use a shared baseClient (axios or fetch wrapper) from src/api/client.ts
Return typed responses using entity types from src/types/
Auth header injected by baseClient interceptor using authScheme from config.ui
auth_rules: AuthGuard wraps every route â€” redirects to login if unauthenticated
requiredRoles on a page maps to actor names from application.json
If the current user's role is not in requiredRoles, render a 403 component, not a redirect

naming_rules.route_file: kebab-case from routePrefix â€” e.g. my-leave, team-approvals
naming_rules.api_file: kebab-module.ts â€” matches module-catalog moduleName kebab
naming_rules.type_file: PascalCase.ts â€” matches entity name from ent-*.json

rules: If requirements or use cases changed, re-derive UI behavior from updated source artifacts before generating files.
When upstream source files changed, regenerate impacted modules/pages/types/api files; do not only run compile or build checks.
A build-only pass is validation, not generation. Do not mark generation complete unless impacted UI artifacts were reconciled with changed requirements.
CRITICAL: config.design.uiModules is the INPUT folder (read-only spec files). config.output.uiSrc is the OUTPUT folder (write all generated code here). Never write into config.design.uiModules.
CRITICAL: Generate .tsx / .ts files only â€” never .ejs, .hbs, .html, .vue, or any other template format unless config.ui.framework explicitly says so.
CRITICAL: The Vite proxy must NOT include a rewrite that strips /api. Backend routes are registered as /api/... so the prefix must be forwarded intact.

CRITICAL: The router must include a default index redirect at the root authenticated route: { index: true, element: <Navigate to='/{first-module-route}' replace /> }. Without this, visiting '/' shows a blank page.
CRITICAL: Never generate duplicate .js and .tsx files for the same component. Generate .tsx only. If legacy .js files exist they will shadow the .tsx and the fix will appear to have no effect.
CRITICAL: All mutation onSuccess handlers must call queryClient.invalidateQueries() for every affected query key before navigating. Without this the list page shows stale cached data after save.

CRITICAL: Error messages shown to users must render the actual server error from error.response.data.error â€” not a hardcoded generic string. Use: (error as any)?.response?.data?.error ?? error?.message ?? 'fallback message'.
Read config.ui for framework and styling choices before generating any file
Read ui-catalog.json menu array to build the router and Sidebar â€” menu order = route order
Read each ui-{module}.json for page specs â€” one component per page
subNav in a module spec â†’ render a TabBar or secondary nav within the module's AppShell slot
navigation.menuVisible=true pages are direct routes; menuVisible=false pages are nested/child routes
Every page component receives typed props â€” no any
Derive column and field names from shows[] and validation[] in the page spec â€” do not invent fields
Every form must implement all validation rules from the page spec
All list pages must render emptyState string when the data array is empty
All delete/destructive actions must show a confirm dialog before proceeding
Do not generate mock data or hardcoded values â€” all data comes from api/ functions
Do not overwrite scaffold files (package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts) if they already exist
Never write outside config.output.uiSrc
The sidebar menu filters items by user role â€” mock auth must default to the highest-privilege role (HR_ADMIN or equivalent) so all modules are visible during development.
tsconfig.json exclude must include src/test to prevent test files from breaking the production build.

Any type used in a page component (e.g. actorName on an audit entry) must be declared in the corresponding type file in src/types/. Cross-check all page field references against the type definitions before generating.

coverage_required_per_page: Displays loading state while data is fetching
Renders correct data when API returns success response
Renders emptyState message when API returns empty array (list pages)
Form submission â€” calls correct API method with correct payload on valid input
Form validation â€” shows field-level error messages when required fields are empty
Form validation â€” shows error when business rule is violated (e.g. end date before start date)
Mutation success â€” navigates to the correct route after successful save/submit
Mutation error â€” displays the server error message from response.data.error (not a hardcoded string)
Confirm modal â€” appears before destructive actions; action is not called unless user confirms
Role-based visibility â€” restricted pages redirect or show 403 when user lacks required role
test.api_mock_pattern: Use vi.mock('@api/{module}') to mock API calls. Return resolved/rejected promises. Never make real HTTP calls in unit tests.
test.query_client_pattern: Wrap each test render in a fresh QueryClient with retry: false to prevent infinite retries on error.
test.router_pattern: Wrap renders in MemoryRouter with initialEntries for route-dependent pages.
test.note: Install @testing-library/react, @testing-library/user-event, @testing-library/jest-dom in devDependencies. Exclude src/test from tsconfig.json to avoid build errors.
